tasks.title: Split require_repoprompt config into rp_plan_required and rp_tool_injection flags
evidence: crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs:108: require_repoprompt is a single Option<bool> field in AgentConfig.
docs/configuration.md:28: require_repoprompt is documented as a single boolean flag.
crates/ralph/src/promptflow.rs:92-112: build_phase1_prompt receives policy.require_repoprompt and passes it to render_worker_phase1_prompt.
User request: Separate into 'repoprompt_plan_required' (injects context_builder planning instructions) and 'repoprompt_tool_injection' (only injects tooling reminders).
plan: Add two new fields to AgentConfig in contracts.rs: repoprompt_plan_required (Option<bool>) and repoprompt_tool_injection (Option<bool>).
Update AgentConfig::merge_from to merge the new fields.
Update Config::default() in contracts.rs to set appropriate defaults for both new fields.
Update the schema (schemas/config.schema.json) to include the two new fields.
Update PromptPolicy in promptflow.rs to have separate fields: repoprompt_plan_required and repoprompt_tool_injection.
Update run_cmd.rs to create PromptPolicy with both flags.
Modify build_phase1_prompt in promptflow.rs to use repoprompt_plan_required for planning instructions and repoprompt_tool_injection for tooling reminders.

Find all other usages of require_repoprompt in promptflow.rs and run_cmd/phases.rs and update logic to use the new flags appropriately (repoprompt_tool_injection for all phases, repoprompt_plan_required for phase 1 planning).
Add tests covering: both flags true, only plan_required true, only tool_injection true, both false.
Update docs/configuration.md to document the new separate flags.
Deprecate but keep require_repoprompt for backward compatibility, mapping it to repoprompt_plan_required for migration.
tasks.request: Separate the repoprompt config settings into 2 configuration settings instead of 1. One config setting for 'repoprompt_plan_required' and one for 'repoprompt_tool_injection'. The first config injects the context_builder planning instructions while the latter only injects the repo prompt tooling reminders.

tasks.title: Add JSON output option for queue stats/history/burndown
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: QueueStatsArgs only defines --tag; QueueHistoryArgs/QueueBurndownArgs only define --days (no format flag).
crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: print_stats/print_history/print_burndown emit text output via println! without JSON serialization.
crates/ralph/tests/queue_stats_history_test.rs: integration tests assert text-only output like "Task Statistics" and "Task Burndown".
docs/cli.md: queue documentation does not describe stats/history/burndown outputs or any JSON format option.
plan: Add a shared --format (text|json) flag to queue stats/history/burndown CLI args and wire it through command dispatch.
Implement JSON output structs/serialization in reports.rs while preserving text as the default.
Update CLI docs/help and adjust queue_stats_history_test.rs to cover both text and JSON output paths.
notes: Current integration tests in queue_stats_history_test.rs are tightly coupled to text output, so they must be updated when JSON support is added.

tasks.title: Normalize tag casing in stats tag breakdown
evidence: crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: print_stats lowercases tags for filtering but the tag breakdown counts tags using their original casing.
crates/ralph/tests/queue_stats_history_test.rs: current integration tests do not assert tag breakdown output or tag casing.
plan: Normalize tag keys to lower-case when aggregating tag_counts in print_stats.
Add a queue_stats_history_test.rs scenario with mixed-case tags and assert the tag breakdown output uses normalized casing and aggregated counts.
Run `cargo test -p ralph queue_stats_history_test` to confirm the report output remains stable.
notes: Tag breakdown casing is currently untested; new assertions should pin the normalized output.

tasks.title: Add module docstrings to integration tests missing file headers

(doctor_contract_test.rs, lock_contention_integration_test.rs, run_cli_overrides_contract_test.rs, timeutil_test.rs, runutil_test.rs, git_repo_integration_test.rs, prompt_cmd_test.rs, fsutil_test.rs, safeguard_integration_test.rs, init_validation_test.rs, stale_lock_cleanup_test.rs, git_safety_test.rs, redaction_expansion_test.rs, config_test.rs, stress_queue_contract_test.rs, contracts_test.rs, scan_cmd_test.rs, gitutil_test.rs, makefile_ci_contract_test.rs).
crates/ralph/tests/task_cmd_test.rs already starts with a //! module docstring, so it no longer needs header work.
plan: Add a concise //! module-level docstring to each file listed in scope (convert existing // file headers to //! where present).
Keep docstrings consistent in tone and describe the test suite purpose in one sentence.
Run the existing test suite to confirm doc comment additions are non-breaking.
notes: makefile_ci_contract_test.rs is newly identified as missing a module docstring; task_cmd_test.rs is already documented and removed from scope.

tasks.title: Allow rejected dependencies to unblock runnable tasks (or update docs/tests to match)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: StatusArg::Rejected help text says dependents can proceed, implying rejected should unblock dependents.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs: are_dependencies_met requires each dependency to have status == TaskStatus::Done (Rejected is treated as unmet).
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/tests.rs: test_next_runnable_task_skips_rejected_dep asserts a rejected dependency blocks runnable selection.
plan: Confirm the intended dependency policy for rejected tasks (unblock dependents vs. keep blocked) by reviewing CLI help expectations and any project guidance.

Align queue dependency logic and comments with the chosen policy (update are_dependencies_met and related error messaging if rejected should satisfy dependencies, or update StatusArg::Rejected help text if it should not).
Adjust/add queue operation tests to cover the chosen behavior and ensure rejected dependency cases remain explicitly verified.
notes: Current code treats rejected dependencies as unmet, which conflicts with the CLI status help text that claims dependents can proceed.

tasks.title: Clarify --force behavior for dirty-repo bypass in run/scan
evidence: docs/cli.md: Global `--force` flag description only calls out bypassing stale queue locks (no mention of clean-repo bypass for run/scan).
docs/cli.md: `ralph run` section notes clean-repo checks and `--force` bypass, but there is no dedicated `ralph scan` section describing clean-repo behavior.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs: run_one_impl calls gitutil::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(..., force, gitutil::RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS).
crates/ralph/src/scan_cmd.rs: run_scan calls gitutil::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(..., opts.force, [".ralph/queue.json", ".ralph/done.json"]).
crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs: after_long_help already documents the clean-repo check and `--force` bypass.
crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs: after_long_help has no clean-repo or `--force` note.
plan: Update docs/cli.md global flag description to mention clean-repo bypass for run/scan, and add a short `ralph scan` section that explains the clean-repo check plus `--force` behavior with examples.
Add a clean-repo/`--force` note to `ralph scan` CLI help text in crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs; confirm run help remains accurate.
Verify help/doc alignment by checking `ralph run --help` and `ralph scan --help` output (or equivalent in docs) before closing.
notes: Run docs/help already mention the clean-repo bypass; the gap is global flag wording and scan documentation/help text.

tasks.title: Make reordering tasks in queue easier by adding functionality to TUI
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/events.rs: Normal-mode key handling only moves selection (Up/Down or j/k) and provides no action to reorder tasks in queue order.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: move_up/move_down only adjust selection/scroll and never mutate queue.tasks, so TUI navigation does not change task order.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: palette_entries lists run/archive/edit/filter/status/priority actions, but no reorder command is exposed.
crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs: help overlay lists navigation/actions/quick changes but does not mention any task reordering shortcut.
plan: Add App methods to move task up/down in queue order (swap in queue.tasks), update filtered indices/selection, set dirty flags, and surface a status message when at boundaries.
Wire new reorder actions into TUI input (events.rs normal mode + palette command wiring in mod.rs) and document keybindings in the help overlay (render.rs).
Add tests covering reorder success and boundary/no-op behavior in the TUI event tests, update help overlay assertions, and run `cargo test -p ralph tui`.
notes: Selection movement already exists via move_up/move_down; reordering will need to operate on queue.tasks while respecting filtered_indices so the visible selection stays coherent.
